Title: The gibberellic acid derived from the plastidial MEP pathway is involved in the accumulation of Bamboo mosaic virus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant developmental processes and, in this study, assists Bamboo mosaic virus accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown of NbGGPPS2 reduced BaMV accumulation, implicating GGPP channeled to GA synthesis in viral accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGGPPS2 knockdown reduced transcript level to about 40% of control. 2. NbGGPPS2 knockdown significantly reduced BaMV coat protein accumulation to about 70% of control. 3. The study links NbGGPPS2-derived GGPP specifically with GA biosynthesis rather than major plastid isoprenoid production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; qRT-PCR; BaMV inoculation; Western blot metabolite/virus accumulation analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in green tissue according to cited comparative background; tested in leaves
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast (inferred from plastidial GGPP synthesis discussed in paper)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host-factor target to reduce BaMV accumulation by limiting GA-linked GGPP supply, though likely with developmental tradeoffs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects GA biosynthesis and related growth processes if strongly reduced
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinguished from NbGGPPS11: only NbGGPPS2, not NbGGPPS11, contributed to BaMV accumulation in this study.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor supply for major plastid isoprenoids such as carotenoids, chlorophylls, tocopherols, phylloquinone and plastoquinone
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown of NbGGPPS11 did not significantly affect BaMV accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGGPPS11 knockdown reduced transcript level to about 60% of control. 2. NbGGPPS11 knockdown caused no significant change in BaMV accumulation. 3. The study uses NbGGPPS11 as a functionally contrasting GGPP synthase isoform relative to NbGGPPS2.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; qRT-PCR; BaMV inoculation; Western blot
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in green tissue according to cited background; tested in leaves
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/chloroplast (inferred from plastidial isoprenoid synthesis role discussed in paper)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely not an effective direct target for altering BaMV accumulation based on this study
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Major plastid isoprenoid pathway gene; stronger perturbation may impair development/photosynthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a tested core comparison gene in the results, but evidence indicates it is not involved in BaMV accumulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant developmental processes and assists BaMV accumulation in this study
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown of NbKS reduced BaMV accumulation, confirming involvement of the GA synthesis pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbKS knockdown reduced transcript level to about 73% of control without obvious morphology change. 2. NbKS knockdown reduced BaMV coat protein accumulation to about 79% of control. 3. This supports that the GA biosynthetic pathway promotes BaMV accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; qRT-PCR; BaMV inoculation; Western blot
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Stroma of proplastids or developing chloroplasts for ent-kaurene synthesis pathway context
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host-factor target to reduce BaMV accumulation by lowering GA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect GA-dependent development if more strongly impaired
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to validate that GA synthesis downstream of GGPP is the relevant branch affecting BaMV accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for IPP/DMAPP and downstream GA biosynthesis that promotes BaMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme in the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant developmental regulation and, in this study, assists BaMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation or overexpression of NbDXR promotes BaMV accumulation, while knockdown or chemical inhibition reduces it
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbDXR was identified as an upregulated host gene after BaMV infection, peaking at 2.6-fold at 1 dpi. 2. VIGS knockdown reduced BaMV CP accumulation to about 53% in leaves and reduced viral CP/RNA in protoplasts. 3. Overexpression of functional NbDXR-T7 increased BaMV CP and RNA about 1.8- and 1.9-fold, respectively. 4. Fosmidomycin inhibition of DXR reduced BaMV accumulation. 5. NbDXR localizes to chloroplasts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; VIGS knockdown; protoplast infection assay; transient overexpression; confocal localization; inhibitor treatment with fosmidomycin; Western blot; Northern blot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently induced after BaMV infection in leaves, highest at 1 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host-factor target for antiviral intervention through MEP-pathway manipulation, though broad isoprenoid effects are likely
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of core MEP metabolism may affect plastid isoprenoid biosynthesis and plant fitness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C-terminal GFP fusion disrupted the positive effect on BaMV accumulation, whereas a small T7 tag preserved function; GA treatment downregulated NbDXR as reported in discussion, suggesting feedback.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBPP to produce IPP and DMAPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the IPP/DMAPP precursor pool for GGPP and downstream GA biosynthesis linked to BaMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant developmental regulation and, in this study, assists BaMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown of NbHDR reduced BaMV accumulation, supporting involvement of MEP-pathway metabolites in viral accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbHDR knockdown reduced transcript abundance to about 58% of control. 2. NbHDR knockdown reduced BaMV coat protein and viral RNA accumulation to about 75% and 69% of control, respectively. 3. This supports the role of downstream MEP-pathway metabolites in promoting BaMV accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; qRT-PCR; BaMV inoculation; Western blot; Northern blot
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antiviral host-pathway target, but broad effects on plastid isoprenoids expected
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Interference with MEP end products can affect essential plastid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NbCMK was also tested upstream/downstream in the pathway, but severe developmental phenotype prevented follow-up functional analysis.
